description: Closed-form golden for the discrete-cadence materialization fixture discrete_materialize_contraction.esm (CONFORMANCE_SPEC.md §5.13). The DISCRETE forcing `src` is piecewise-constant across the cadence segments, so the materialized contraction `g[j] = sum_i W[i,j]*src[i]` and the integrated trajectory are exact. `forcing.by_anchor` are the OFFLINE src snapshots written into each binding's forcing buffer at the matching `refresh_times` anchor (no regrid — delivered directly on the sim index). `discrete_field.M.g` is g re-materialized at each anchor from that snapshot (the discrete cache contents); `const_field.M.k` is the CONST-cadence sibling, refresh-invariant. `trajectory` is the segmented closed-form integral of D(c[j]) = g[j] + k[j]. Every simulation binding reproduces `trajectory` (trajectory band); a materializing binding (Julia) additionally reproduces `discrete_field`/`const_field` and asserts the const/discrete classification.

weights.comment: W[i,j], i=1..2 (rows), j=1..3 (cols). The const mixing weights of the contraction.

M.src.comment: src[i], i=1..2, refreshed at each anchor. Distinct paired values per anchor make the contraction load-bearing: a stale (un-refreshed) src gives a visibly wrong trajectory slope.

const_field.comment: k[j] = sum_i W[i,j]*offset with offset=1: [1+4, 2+5, 3+6]. Refresh-invariant (never a per-refresh cache).

discrete_field.comment: g[j] = sum_i W[i,j]*src[i] re-materialized at each anchor (the discrete cache contents).
